title: Understand: cell parts and functions
description: Check your understanding of cell parts and functions in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What do all organelles have in common?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They all have a specific function in the cell.
They all release energy for the cell to use.
They all contain the organism’s genes.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is true about the role of organelles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Each organelle carries out a specific function for the cell.
All organelles release usable forms of energy.
All organelles help the cell hold its shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is true about how organelles are unique?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Each organelle performs a different job in the cell.
Some organelles are structural and others are functional.
Each organelle contains a different set of genes.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word or phrase to fill in the blank.**

>The function of \_\_\_\_\_\_ is to contain an organism’s genes.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the nucleus
mitochondria
a chloroplast

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which organelle breaks down sugars to release energy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: mitochondria
chloroplasts
the nucleus

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which organelle does photosynthesis occur?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: chloroplasts
mitochondria
the nucleus

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statements about the cell membrane are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cell membrane surrounds all contents of the cell.
The cell membrane lets certain molecules into the cell and keeps other molecules out.
The cell membrane prevents all substances from leaving the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a main function of the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to surround all contents of the cell
to prevent all substances from entering the cell
to ensure organelles can leave the cell

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a main function of the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to let nutrients in and waste out
to surround some organelles, but not all of them
to contain and organize the organism’s genes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following cell parts is present in both plant *and* animal cells?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: mitochondria
chloroplasts
a cell wall

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which cell parts are found in plant cells, but not animal cells?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: mitochondria
chloroplasts
a cell wall

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following cell parts is present in both plant *and* animal cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a nucleus
chloroplasts
a cell wall
